paragraphs.body: Imagine a wall painted in many bright, beautiful colors, each color representing different feelings, desires, and ways of loving. Society, however, usually insists on seeing love as just one single color, ignoring the richness and variety of human emotions. The rainbow flag is different—it proudly displays many colors, representing how diverse love can be. It tells us that love is beautiful because it is diverse, and people should be free to express their true feelings openly, without fear or shame. This flag symbolizes bravery, urging us to be ourselves and to accept others for who they truly are.

annotations.text: This flag symbolizes bravery, urging us to be ourselves and to accept others for who they truly are.
annotations.comment: Nice line. I wonder, though, if these summary ideas would be more powerful later in the essay? What if you started your essay with the narrative?

paragraphs.body: One warm June evening, I walked down a crowded street filled with music, laughter, and vibrant colors. Rainbow flags waved proudly everywhere, representing freedom and pride. Among the festive crowd, I noticed two couples who stood out to me. One couple held hands tightly, their fingers trembling slightly as they walked. One person seemed anxious, eyes constantly looking around, nervous about how others viewed them. Their partner, however, walked confidently, holding tightly and looking straight ahead. Close by, another couple seemed lost in their own world, smiling and completely focused on each other. Yet, as I overheard bits of their conversation earlier, I realized their relationship was not as simple as it appeared. They had started deeply in love, completely devoted to each other, but now faced difficulty because one of them had developed feelings for someone else. The happiness on their faces was mixed with quiet worry and sadness. Seeing these couples taught me something important: love isn't always straightforward. It takes courage to stay true to your feelings, especially when your emotions don't fit what society expects.

annotations.text: It takes courage to stay true to your feelings,
annotations.comment: interesting! I wonder if this idea is important to you and your own as well? Let's talk :)

annotations.text: One couple held hands tightly, their fingers trembling slightly as they walked. One person seemed anxious, eyes constantly looking around, nervous about how others viewed them.

paragraphs.body: Love is usually defined simply as romantic attraction or strong affection for another person. But this basic definition does not fully capture what love really means. Love involves more than just physical attraction it includes deep emotional connections, shared values, understanding, and genuine caring. At the same time, our natural instincts, driven by biology and evolution, often push us toward multiple people. Society usually promotes a very strict view of relationships: one person should love one partner exclusively. But reality is different. Many people find themselves attracted to multiple people. Society often labels this as wrong or unnatural. People facing such desires must choose either to deny their true feelings or hide them from their partners. Both choices involve dishonesty and create unhappiness. Does this mean that people who feel love differently from society's expectations do not deserve happiness and love?

annotations.text: Both choices involve dishonesty and create unhappiness. Does this mean that people who feel love differently from society's expectations do not deserve happiness and love?

paragraphs.body: True love, at its heart, means being honest and open with each other. Love loses its purity when people feel forced to lie or hide their true selves due to societal pressure. Relationships based on dishonesty become painful and difficult. Real love grows best in an environment of openness, trust, and mutual respect. Being honest and open about feelings is risky and can lead to being judged or rejected, but it also leads to genuine happiness and emotional freedom. Thus, the purest love isn't about fitting into society's strict rules, but about having the courage to be truthful about who we are and how we feel.

annotations.text: Love loses its purity when people feel forced to lie or hide their true selves due to societal pressure.
annotations.comment: Again, how do these ideas relate to you, specifically? You seem to be summarizing a world view rather than making specific connections to your own life and experience.

paragraphs.body: Love is complicated and powerful, bringing happiness but also causing pain. It is hard to know if people would still love if they always thought logically. Philosopher Schopenhauer believed that pain stays with us longer and deeper than happiness. We easily remember times we were hurt, but quickly forget moments of joy. In his novel "The Unbearable Lightness of Being," Milan Kundera also explores how love can feel both heavy and light at the same time, meaningful yet temporary. Our natural instincts push us toward love, creating both warmth and uncertainty. If true love is that torture, why don’t me act like the rainbow flag, a symbol of bravery, authenticity, and resistance against strict societal rules about love and relationships.

annotations.text: Philosopher Schopenhauer believed that pain stays with us longer and deeper than happiness. We easily remember times we were hurt, but quickly forget moments of joy. In his novel "The Unbearable Lightness of Being," Milan Kundera also explores how love can feel both heavy and light at the same time, meaningful yet temporary.
annotations.comment: lovely. We have some ideas to talk about! What is your experience of love?

paragraphs.body: Thinking back to the couples I saw that evening, I admire their bravery. Holding hands openly on that crowded street was not just an expression of affection, it was an act of courage. They were bravely being themselves, even though society might not fully accept them. Their actions perfectly illustrated the message of the rainbow flag: be brave, authentic, and true to yourself, despite what society might think. Their courage was inspiring, showing how love can survive and thrive even when it faces difficulties or judgments from Ultimately, love cannot be easily defined because it is complex, involving many layers of emotions, desires, and choices. Society often feels uncomfortable with relationships that don't follow traditional norms. Yet, accepting different ways of loving can enrich our communities and help us understand each other better. Like the rainbow flag, love should be celebrated in all its colors and forms, free from unnecessary restrictions. Everyone deserves the chance to express love openly and honestly, without fear of judgment or exclusion. The bravery to love authentically, embracing all complexities, allows love to exist in its most real and beautiful form.

annotations.text: Thinking back to the couples I saw that evening, I admire their bravery. Holding hands openly on that crowded street was not just an expression of affection, it was an act of courage.
